title: Analyst Assurance Desk
public_claim: An analyst-assurance workstation for human review of AI-supported intelligence tasks.
distinctive_mechanism: It makes override decisions, analyst-model disagreement, task-family calibration, source review, structured analytic technique outputs, uncertainty, assumptions, reasoning paths, scoring rules, and release chronology visible as native objects.
use_value: It is meant to show what human-in-the-loop review actually requires: not trust in a reviewer, but surfaces that let the reviewer inspect sources, assumptions, uncertainty, reasoning paths, and machine-aided judgments before release.

component_proofs.claim_supported: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
component_proofs.input: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
component_proofs.expected_output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Task And Method Assignment; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: separates evidence from assumptions before the analytic method begins; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_proofs.input: source record, assumptions, alternatives, indicators, confidence basis, and possible deception conditions
component_proofs.expected_output: evidence/assumption ledger as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: evidence/assumption ledger as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Evidence And Assumption Intake; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: turns competing explanations into an inspectable matrix instead of a narrative preference
component_proofs.input: hypotheses, evidence rows, diagnosticity, contradiction, and consistency judgments
component_proofs.expected_output: ACH-style comparison product as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: ACH-style comparison product as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through ACH / Alternatives Matrix; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: attacks the current judgment before it is polished into final prose; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_proofs.input: favored conclusion, assumptions, missing evidence, rival frame, and adversary incentives
component_proofs.expected_output: challenge memo and revision demand as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: challenge memo and revision demand as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Red-Team / Devil's Advocate Pass; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: prevents a fluent answer from presenting uncertainty as stronger than the evidence supports
component_proofs.input: confidence level, source quality, ambiguity, contradiction, and collection gaps
component_proofs.expected_output: confidence statement and collection-gap list as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: confidence statement and collection-gap list as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Uncertainty And Confidence Gate; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: shows where AI helped, failed, hallucinated, over-weighted, or missed analyst context
component_proofs.input: AI output, human analyst judgment, source trail, uncertainty basis, and override reason
component_proofs.expected_output: accept, qualify, block, rerun, or quarantine decision
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: accept, qualify, block, rerun, or quarantine decision
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Human-AI Delta Review; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: tests which SAT sequence is useful for a specific research or intelligence task and which combinations add cost without value
component_proofs.input: multiple SATs, task type, stage of inquiry, and analyst burden
component_proofs.expected_output: SAT sequence recommendation and warning as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: SAT sequence recommendation and warning as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Technique Combination Studio; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

component_proofs.claim_supported: turns one analytic exercise into a reusable record for improving future human-AI analytic workflows
component_proofs.input: final analytic product, method trail, disagreement state, review decision, and lessons learned
component_proofs.expected_output: release decision and method-learning record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: release decision and method-learning record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Release And Learning Ledger; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

minimum_public_demo: Trace one Russia claim through Task And Method Assignment; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
